title: cerastium vulgatum var. beeringianum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum var. beeringianum, also known as the alpine chickweed or common chickweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Cerastium vulgatum var. beeringianum can be made into infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
